papers.title: Postponing Retirement , Endogenous Fertility , Social Security
papers.llm_reason: Retirement age and social security affect fertility

papers.title: Child-care Policies and Pension in an Endogenous Fertility Model
papers.llm_reason: Childcare and pensions have opposing fertility effects

papers.title: Child-Care Policies in Pay-As-You-Go Pension
papers.llm_reason: Studies PAYG pensions as explicit causal driver of fertility in OLG model with endogenous fertility choice; finds child-care subsidies offset negative fertility effect of pensions, directly testing pension-fertility mechanism.

papers.title: The Costs of Increasing the Fertility Rate in an Endogenous Growth Model
papers.llm_reason: Studies how pension system generosity affects the cost of fertility policy; finding that generous PAYG pensions increase the cost of fertility expansion implies pensions reduce fertility incentives, supporting the hypothesis causal direction.

papers.title: How a Pay-As-You-Go Pension System Can Lead To a Pareto Improvement in an Overlapping Generations Model with Endogenous Fertility
papers.llm_reason: OLG model with endogenous fertility and PAYG pensions, but primary research question is welfare analysis rather than whether pensions causally reduce fertility; pension-fertility link may be a third-order equilibrium byproduct rather than explicit driver.

papers.title: Public expenditure on health and private old-age insurance in an OLG model
papers.llm_reason: Studies formal old-age insurance as explicit causal driver of fertility in OLG model with endogenous fertility; directly tests old-age security arrangements' effect on fertility, matching hypothesis focus (though finding shows insurance increases rather than decreases fertility).

papers.title: Pension Policy Reform and Fertility: Micro Evidence from Ghana
papers.llm_reason: Uses natural experiment (pension reform) to estimate causal effect of PAYG pension introduction on fertility; explicitly identifies old-age security motive as mechanism, finding pension reduces fertility particularly for women relying on children for old-age support.
